questions.question: What is a quadrilateral?
questions.correct_answer: A polygon with four sides and four angles
questions.explanation: It is a closed figure with four straight edges and vertices.

questions.question: Name the types of quadrilaterals based on sides.
questions.correct_answer: Square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezium, kite
questions.explanation: Quadrilaterals are classified based on sides and angles.

questions.question: What is a parallelogram?
questions.correct_answer: A quadrilateral with opposite sides parallel and equal
questions.explanation: Opposite sides and opposite angles are equal in a parallelogram.

questions.question: State the properties of a rectangle.
questions.correct_answer: All angles are 90°, opposite sides equal and parallel
questions.explanation: A rectangle is a special parallelogram with right angles.

questions.question: State the properties of a square.
questions.correct_answer: All sides equal, all angles 90°, opposite sides parallel
questions.explanation: A square is a rectangle with equal sides or a rhombus with right angles.

questions.question: What is a rhombus?
questions.correct_answer: A quadrilateral with all sides equal and opposite sides parallel
questions.explanation: A rhombus may have non-right angles; diagonals bisect each other at right angles.

questions.question: What is a trapezium?
questions.correct_answer: A quadrilateral with at least one pair of opposite sides parallel
questions.explanation: Also called trapezoid in some countries.

questions.question: What is a kite?
questions.correct_answer: A quadrilateral with two pairs of adjacent sides equal
questions.explanation: One diagonal is the axis of symmetry and diagonals intersect at right angles.

questions.question: State the relation between diagonals and sides in a rectangle.
questions.correct_answer: Diagonals are equal and bisect each other
questions.explanation: This property helps in solving problems related to diagonals.

questions.question: State the relation between diagonals and sides in a rhombus.
questions.correct_answer: Diagonals bisect each other at right angles
questions.explanation: Diagonals are not necessarily equal, but they bisect angles and each other perpendicularly.

notes.title: Quadrilateral Basics
notes.content: A quadrilateral has four sides and four angles; sum of angles = 360°.

notes.title: Types of Quadrilaterals
notes.content: Square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezium, kite – classified by sides and angles.

notes.title: Properties of Parallelogram
notes.content: Opposite sides and opposite angles are equal; diagonals bisect each other.

notes.title: Rectangle and Square
notes.content: Rectangle: all angles 90°, diagonals equal. Square: all sides equal, all angles 90°, diagonals equal and perpendicular.

notes.title: Diagonals of Rhombus
notes.content: Diagonals bisect each other at right angles; diagonals bisect opposite angles.

facts.fact: Sum of interior angles of any quadrilateral = 360°.
Diagonals of a rectangle are equal and bisect each other.
Diagonals of a rhombus are perpendicular bisectors of each other.
Square is both a rectangle and a rhombus.
Trapezium has only one pair of opposite sides parallel.
